  • Who am I?
  • CV

    Hi there!

    I'm Zofia, but everyone calls me Zosia (Zoh-see-ah, or 조시아). I'm a second-year master's student at KAIST, studying Industrial Design. I work in the field of Human-Computer Interaction with Professor Andrea Bianchi, and my research focuses on digital fabrication and crafting technologies.

    I'm all about finding ways to bring traditional crafting and modern tech together. In my work, I want to help crafters digitize their processes, making it easier to record and share their work in new and exciting ways. I love exploring how technology can fit seamlessly into creative workflows without taking away the personal touch.

    I'm passionate about designing tools that are intuitive and meaningful for the crafting community. If you're into crafting, digital fabrication, or just love talking, let's connect—I'd love to chat!

    I spend my free time crocheting (duh), solving sudokus, doing puzzles, and bouldering. For a bit more adrenaline, I travel the world with my older sister.
